steps.title: Compute boundary values
steps.content:
All four tables use $x=3$, $x=5$, and $x=8$. Substitute those values into the boundary expression:
$$
13(3)-18=21
$$
$$
13(5)-18=47
$$
$$
13(8)-18=86
$$

steps.title: Test the inequality
steps.content:
For every row, the table's $y$-value must be greater than the matching boundary value. The table with $y$-values $26$, $52$, and $91$ works because
$$
26\gt21,\quad 52\gt47,\quad 91\gt86
$$

steps.title: Identify the table
steps.content: That table is the only one whose $y$-value is above $13x-18$ in all three rows. The answer is D.
